content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://www.ibm.com/think/topics/rlhf", "source_title": "What Is Reinforcement Learning From Human Feedback (RLHF)?", "source_type": "web", "source_quality": 0.7, "overall_relevance": 0.65, "year": 0, "authors": [], "venue": "", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Moreover, sparse success signals are not well suited for agent learning, often necessitating reward shaping (Ng et al., 1999), where the rew...", "direct_quote": "Moreover, sparse success signals are not well suited for agent learning, often necessitating reward shaping (Ng et al., 1999), where the reward signal is transformed into one that is more suitable for learning.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "This can lead to reward hacking (Skalse et al., 2022), where agents exploit such correlations to maximize rewards while producing undesired ...", "direct_quote": "This can lead to reward hacking (Skalse et al., 2022), where agents exploit such correlations to maximize rewards while producing undesired outcomes.", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}, {"statement": "RLHF has seen a number of successful applications, methodological advances, and theoretical insights since the last comparable survey (Wirth...", "direct_quote": "RLHF has seen a number of successful applications, methodological advances, and theoretical insights since the last comparable survey (Wirth et al., 2017).", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.65, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Scientific", "entities": []}], "evidence_summary": "Auto-extracted 3 facts."}]}
